candidates.stmt:
Ed Skeates is standing for Horsham District Council to champion practical, community‑led solutions for Slinfold, Itchingfield and Warnham. With a background in  housing and finance, he brings a clear focus on affordability, sustainability and protecting the character of our villages. Ed is committed to transparent decision‑making, supporting local services, and ensuring development works for residents rather than being imposed on them. He believes in constructive collaboration and giving communities a stronger voice in shaping their future. Ed lives locally and is a parish councillor in Slinfold and wants to strengthen the resilience, fairness and wellbeing of our rural parishes.  In particular he will focus on:

Housing
Horsham needs new homes, but they must be the right homes in the right places. The Liberal Democrats believe development should come with the infrastructure families need — including GP surgeries, school places and transport links. We will prioritise genuinely affordable homes for local people while protecting the character of our towns and villages. Ed chaired the Slinfold Neighbourhood Plan and successfully challenged developers to reduce proposed housing numbers down from initially over 700 to a final allocation of 77 homes in Slinfold Parish.

Improving River Quality
Our rivers and streams should be safe, clean and thriving for wildlife and residents alike. The Liberal Democrats are campaigning to end sewage dumping and hold water companies accountable for pollution. As a keen canoeist , Ed will push for stronger monitoring, tougher penalties, and real investment so our local waterways can recover.

Protecting Green Spaces
Green spaces are part of what makes Horsham such a wonderful place to live. The Liberal Democrats will fight to protect parks, countryside and wildlife habitats from inappropriate development. By safeguarding these spaces and expanding access to nature, we can ensure future generations enjoy a greener, healthier Horsham.  In S

I'm your Green Party candidate for Horsham District Council in the Itchingfield, Slinfold and Warnham ward by-election and in the Broadbridge WSCC election.
You face an important choice when you go to vote on 7th May. That choice is between the same old parties doing the same old things, or having a representative
on the Council who really represents you. I'm standing against cuts to school funding, for adequate provision of school places and for proper funding for our NHS and social care. I'm standing against building thousands of homes across precious countryside without any of the infrastructure for the people who live there. I'm standing for a reliable rail service that enables people in Horsham to live and work, rather than just work and commute.

I’m local, hard-working, and I have the skills to do the job. You may recognise my name. In the last three general elections, I've been your Green Party parliamentary candidate. I've lived in Horsham District since I was two years old. I run my business here, I'm raising my kids here, I use local services and green spaces just like you do. 
As someone who grew up in one of Horsham's villages and who now lives in another one, I get fed up when our villages are ignored and neglected, so I'm standing up to do something about it.

I am always free to speak up for local residents. Unlike other parties, the Green Party doesn't tell councillors how to vote. I can always put you ahead of party politics and stand up for what's right for Barns Green, Itchingfield, Strood Green, Slinfold, Kingsfold, Warnham and of course Broadbridge.

We all know our area is being overdeveloped. Expensive new houses are built on green fields instead of genuinely affordable houses that local people can rent or buy. Instead of creating barriers across our town and its villages, I want to create opportunities.
